steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: Before the blank, “Exemplars of Stigler's law abound” is a complete independent clause. After the blank, the sentence introduces a series of examples from different fields.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule for introducing elaborations
steps.content:
A colon introduces a list or elaboration that follows an independent clause.

Option B (“abound: in philosophy,”) correctly places the colon right after the main clause to introduce the examples.

Options A and D misuse other punctuation (semicolon and misplaced colon), while Option C uses commas inappropriately.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
